message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) has become a serious problem for public health since it was identified in the province of Wuhan (China) and spread around the world producing high mortality rates and economic losses. Nowadays, WHO recognizes traditional, complementary, and alternative medicine for treating COVID-19 symptoms. Therefore, we investigated the antiviral potential of the hydroalcoholic extract of
                  <jats:italic>Uncaria tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  stem bark from Peru against SARS-CoV-2
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  . The antiviral activity of
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  against SARS-CoV-2
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  was assessed in Vero E6 cells using cytopathic effect (CPE) and plaque reduction assay. After 48h of treatment,
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  showed an inhibition of 92.7 % of SARS-CoV-2 at 25.0 µg/mL (p<0.0001) by plaque reduction assay on Vero E6 cells. In addition,
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  , induced a reduction of 98.6 % (p=0.02) and 92.7 % (p=0.03) in the CPE caused by SARS-CoV-2 on Vero E6 cells at 25 µg/mL and 12.5 µg/mL, respectively. The EC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  calculated for
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  extract by plaque reduction assay was 6.6 µg/mL (4.89 – 8.85 µg/mL) for a selectivity index of 4.1. The EC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  calculated for
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  extract by TCID
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  assay was 2.57 µg/mL (1.05 – 3.75 µg/mL) for a selectivity index of 10.54. These results showed that
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  known as Cat’s claw has antiviral effect against SARS-CoV-2 observed as a reduction in the viral titer and CPE after 48h of treatment on Vero E6 cells. Therefore, we hypothesized that
                  <jats:italic>U. tomentosa</jats:italic>
                  stem bark, could be promissory to the development of new therapeutic strategies against SARS-CoV-2.
                </jats:p>
title: The hydroalcoholic extract of Uncaria tomentosa (Cat’s claw) inhibits the replication of novel coronavirus (SARS-CoV-2) in vitro
